title: Floating particle galaxy
desc: Thousands of particles forming a swirling galaxy with mouse-reactive depth and bloom glow.
prompt:
Build a full-viewport Three.js hero section with:
- 8,000 particles in a galaxy spiral (3 arms)
- Depth-based size and opacity (parallax feel)
- Mouse move rotates the camera slightly (lerped)
- Soft purple/blue color gradient across arms
- requestAnimationFrame loop, auto-resize
- Cap renderer.setPixelRatio at 2
- Reduced-motion: pause rotation if prefers-reduced-motion

title: Morphing blob geometry
desc: An organic sphere that breathes and morphs using Perlin noise displacement on vertices.
prompt:
Create a Three.js animated blob hero in a single HTML file:
- IcosahedronGeometry (detail 6) with vertex displacement
- Perlin/simplex noise applied to each vertex per frame
- Iridescent MeshPhysicalMaterial (transmission, roughness 0.1)
- 3-point HDRI-style lighting rig
- Subtle mouse-tracking rotation (lerp factor 0.05)
- Black background, bloom post-processing via UnrealBloomPass
- Mobile: halve geometry detail on screens < 768px

title: Scroll-driven 3D tunnel
desc: A neon tunnel that the camera flies through as the user scrolls down the page.
prompt:
Build a scroll-driven Three.js tunnel hero:
- TubeGeometry along a CatmullRom curve (50 points, radius 2)
- Camera position mapped to window.scrollY (0 → end of tube)
- Neon grid texture on tube interior (ShaderMaterial)
- Emissive glow rings every 10 units along the tube
- Page content layered above canvas with mix-blend-mode
- Pause animation when tab is hidden (visibilitychange)
- Works from 320px to 2560px wide

title: CSS perspective card stack
desc: Layered cards that fan out and lift on hover using pure CSS perspective transforms.
prompt:
Build a pure-CSS 3D card-stack hero section:
- 5 stacked cards with translateZ, rotateX, rotateY offsets
- Hover on the stack fans cards out in 3D space (CSS transitions 0.5s ease)
- Each card has a glassmorphism surface (backdrop-filter blur)
- Perspective 1200px set on the parent container
- transform-style: preserve-3d throughout
- Responsive: flatten to 2D on prefers-reduced-motion
- No JavaScript required

title: 3D text extrusion hero
desc: Headline text with a CSS 3D extrusion effect that reacts to mouse position.
prompt:
Create a CSS 3D extruded-text hero:
- Headline text with 20 layered text-shadows simulating extrusion depth
- Mouse move updates CSS custom properties --rx and --ry
- The text block rotates via rotateX(var(--rx)) rotateY(var(--ry))
- Extrusion colour is 30% darker than the face colour
- Smooth perspective tilt (lerp in requestAnimationFrame)
- Font: system-ui or a single Google Font import
- Reduced-motion: disable tilt, keep static extrusion

title: Interactive fluid simulation
desc: Silky fluid that ripples and splashes wherever the cursor moves using 2D canvas.
prompt:
Implement a 2D canvas fluid-ripple hero background:
- Grid of velocity/density cells (128×128 resolution)
- Mouse drag injects dye and velocity into the field
- Advection + diffusion solved each frame (Jos Stam-style)
- Render cells as coloured rectangles scaled to viewport
- 60 fps locked with requestAnimationFrame + delta capping
- Purple → teal colour map for density values
- Mobile: respond to touchmove events instead of mousemove

title: Generative wave landscape
desc: Stacked sine-wave mountain ridges in a looping canvas animation.
prompt:
Build a canvas 2D animated landscape hero:
- 6 layered sine-wave ridgelines, each with different frequency/amplitude/speed
- Ridges filled with semi-transparent gradients (dark purple at top → transparent)
- Ridges scroll horizontally at different speeds (parallax depth)
- Stars layer: 200 static dots with twinkle opacity animation
- Entire scene loops seamlessly (x offset wraps at wave repeat width)
- Add a subtle radial gradient moon/sun behind the ridges
- requestAnimationFrame loop, canvas fills 100vw × 100vh

title: Embed a Spline 3D scene
desc: Load a Spline scene via the runtime SDK and sync it with scroll or cursor.
prompt:
Show me how to embed a Spline 3D scene as a hero section:
- Import @splinetool/runtime via CDN
- Load a scene URL into a full-viewport <canvas>
- Map window.scrollY to a Spline variable named "scrollProgress"
- Map mousemove to Spline variables "mouseX" and "mouseY"
- Show a loading skeleton until the scene is ready
- Make the canvas position: fixed behind page content
- Include a fallback static image for browsers without WebGL

title: GSAP ScrollTrigger 3D reveal
desc: Section elements fly in from 3D space as the user scrolls, orchestrated by GSAP.
prompt:
Build a GSAP ScrollTrigger hero with 3D reveals:
- Hero headline splits into words, each flies in from rotateX(90deg) → 0
- Sub-heading fades from opacity 0 + translateZ(-80px) → normal
- CTA button scales from 0.6 → 1 with a spring ease
- A background mesh (SVG or canvas) parallaxes at 30% scroll speed
- Use gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger) from CDN
- All animations use perspective on the parent (1000px)
- Stagger word reveals by 0.06s each

title: Holographic product display
desc: A rotating 3D object with a holographic shader, grid lines, and scan animation.
prompt:
Build a Three.js holographic product-display hero:
- Load a GLB model (or use a TorusKnotGeometry as placeholder)
- Custom ShaderMaterial with:
    • Fresnel edge glow (rim lighting)
    • Horizontal scan-line animation moving upward
    • Grid overlay in UV space
    • Iridescent colour shift based on view angle
- Model rotates slowly on Y axis
- Click/drag to orbit (OrbitControls, damping enabled)
- Dark background with a faint circular ground shadow
- Export as a single HTML file with CDN imports

title: Infinite 3D logo cube
desc: A CSS cube rotating in 3D with each face showing a different brand asset or gradient.
prompt:
Create a CSS 3D rotating cube logo hero:
- A cube with 6 faces, each a different gradient or brand colour
- Auto-rotates on X and Y axes simultaneously (CSS @keyframes)
- Face labels ("About", "Work", "Contact", etc.) centred on each face
- Hover pauses rotation and highlights the nearest face
- Cube size: 160px on desktop, 100px on mobile
- transform-style: preserve-3d, perspective: 600px on wrapper
- Clicking a face dispatches a custom event with the face label

title: Matrix rain with custom glyphs
desc: The classic falling-code rain, customised with your own characters and colours.
prompt:
Create a canvas Matrix-rain hero background:
- Columns of falling characters (mix of katakana + Latin + digits)
- Each column has random speed (1–3 cells/frame) and start delay
- Lead character is bright white; trail fades to your accent colour
- Background: black with 0.05 alpha fill each frame (motion blur effect)
- Font: monospace 14px
- On mousemove, columns near cursor temporarily turn accent2 colour
- Add a CSS overlay gradient (transparent → bg colour) at the bottom
